title: App Entry Point (app/page.tsx)
content:
This file serves as the main entry point for the DocMed application's home page. It orchestrates the rendering of various UI components to create a comprehensive landing experience.

## Purpose

The primary purpose of this file is to define the structure and content of the home page. It imports and arranges components such as the Navbar, Hero section, Trust Bar, Features, How It Works section, Statistics, Call to Action, and Footer.

## Key Components and Exports

-   **`Navbar`**: A functional React component responsible for rendering the application's header, including navigation links, a logo, and a mobile-responsive menu.
-   **`Hero`**: A functional React component that displays the main hero section with a rotating globe animation, a compelling headline, and call-to-action buttons.
-   **`TrustBar`**: A functional React component that showcases key technologies and integrations used by DocMed, presented as a simple horizontal bar.
-   **`Features`**: A functional React component that details the core features of DocMed, each presented with an icon, title, description, and a highlight.
-   **`HowItWorks`**: A functional React component that outlines the step-by-step process of using DocMed, from connecting repositories to AI-driven documentation updates.
-   **`Stats`**: A functional React component that displays key statistics and metrics related to DocMed's effectiveness.
-   **`CTA`**: A functional React component that implements a Call to Action section, encouraging users to sign up, featuring an email input and a submit button.
-   **`Footer`**: A functional React component rendering the application's footer, typically containing copyright information and a logo.
-   **`Home` (default export)**: The main `page.tsx` component that renders all the above sections sequentially to form the complete home page.

## Usage Example

This file is the root component for the `/` route. Its components are automatically rendered when a user navigates to the home page of the DocMed application. The `Home` component itself is the default export:

```typescript
// app/page.tsx

export default function Home() {
  return (
    <main>
      <Navbar />
      <Hero />
      <TrustBar />
      <Features />
      <HowItWorks />
      <Stats />
      <CTA />
      <Footer />
    </main>
  );
}
```

Each of these sub-components (`Navbar`, `Hero`, etc.) are defined within this file or imported from other modules (e.g., `@/components/ui/globe-hero`) and are responsible for their respective UI sections.
